strengths: 15+ years of full-stack depth with TypeScript and Node.js as recurring production tools across multiple companies (re:cinq, Optilyz, RIDE Capital)
Active AI agent infrastructure work at current role — MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks directly match the AI agent development requirement
Strong testing culture evidenced by TDD practices at RIDE, own testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and multiple public workshops on testing and TDD
concerns: NestJS is the primary backend framework for this role and is entirely absent from the resume — no mention of it in any capacity
Kotlin/Java experience is shallow and dated — limited to robotic arm work at Altom (2016–2018) with no production JVM backend services
Vitest and Playwright are not mentioned; testing framework fluency is listed as a hard requirement and cannot be inferred from older Jest/DLang-era experience
response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine full-stack seniority: TypeScript and Node.js appear as production tools across four consecutive roles, AWS CDK and MongoDB are documented at Optilyz, Docker and Kubernetes at GISCollective, and his current re:cinq role explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure including MCP frameworks — a differentiator few candidates will have. The primary gap is NestJS, which is the stated backend framework and is nowhere on the resume; for a mid-senior hire expected to contribute to architecture decisions immediately, this is a real ramp-up cost. Kotlin/Java exposure is too narrow and old to satisfy the secondary stack requirement. These are learnable gaps for a developer of this calibre, but they are real. The AI agent work and TypeScript depth outweigh the framework mismatch enough to warrant a conversation.
